behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in one lint rule’s text-match/AST logic, not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text "static prompt-template lint check" (no harness/runtime/model dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with template fixtures containing and omitting phase-instruction snippets copied from SKILL.md; assert reject/accept decisions and violation spans.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at cross-module contract (template interpolation + fixture injection + final prompt builder). Unit tests on template fragments can miss leakage introduced during assembly. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no model-output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline with production-like fixtures; assert the emitted final prompt contains no phase-instruction strings/patterns from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: concrete boundary is loader->harness state handoff (skill-file content must flow into invocation context). A unit test of loader alone cannot prove invocation uses loaded context instead of hard-coded rules. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real network/subprocess needed; local files are sufficient; (b) this behavior can be validated without judging model quality; (c) no multi-step agent pipeline required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test setup with real plugin/skill file loading, then assert invocation context references loaded skill-derived data and no hard-coded phase rules are present in prompt config.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in wiring between variant selection, skill-file resolution, and prompt immutability contract (same prompt text across runs). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) does not require real network/subprocess; (b) can be checked without relying on model output correctness; (c) no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness twice (baseline skill vs degraded/missing variant) with identical user prompt fixture; assert prompt text is byte-identical while skill source path/variant changes are applied.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on what the real model does with changed skill context under the same prompt; stubbed responses would hide this failure. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior text describes one prompt-run comparison, not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live-model behavioral smoke test with unchanged user prompt for baseline vs degraded skill variant; assert degraded variant violates target behavior and test fails.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering invariant across modules (model client output -> artifact writer -> parser). Unit parser tests cannot catch pre-parse capture ordering regressions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) local artifact write is enough; no real network/subprocess behavior is the failure boundary; (b) does not depend on model semantic quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Inject representative raw completion text via test double/model client, run harness path, and assert diagnostic artifact is written with raw text before parser/extractor invocation timestamps/events.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is assertion-rule logic itself (structure validator vs keyword checker), primarily local function/object behavior. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection gate: Integration+ disqualified because behavior text is about assertion semantics, not runtime wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion utilities with crafted outputs: cases that contain expected keywords but wrong schema/role placement must fail; correctly structured outputs must pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and regression detection depends on real model outputs that vary and can be superficially keyword-correct while structurally wrong. Rejection gate for Workflow: text does not require multiple sequential model decisions, only live execution plus one evaluation stage.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompts known to produce occasional structurally-invalid but keyword-matching responses; assert structural validator fails these cases while keyword-only baseline would pass.
